summary: The papers focus on advancing small UAS capabilities and countermeasures through diverse technical approaches. One paper develops a 3D LiDAR-based tracking system using adaptive Extended Kalman Filtering for precise relative positioning in swarm robotics, emphasizing robustness under varying lighting. Another explores visual cooperative tracking combined with open-path Tunable Diode Laser Absorption Spectroscopy for real-time gas concentration mapping, leveraging in-situ and tomographic sensing. A third presents a multi-physics simulation framework for designing high-power microwave counter-UAS systems, targeting non-kinetic neutralization of autonomous small drones resistant to RF jamming. A fourth introduces Diamond, a lightweight authenticated encryption framework tailored for resource-constrained IoT devices like small drones, ensuring secure telemetry transmission under tight energy and computing limits. Finally, a paper proposes YolovN-CBi, a modified lightweight neural network architecture optimized for real-time detection of small, fast-moving UAVs despite their low visual contrast.
